pi_items.source: Verified by cross-referencing with ci_pipeline_for_flask_api task setup scripts that make API calls to /TailwindTraders/ endpoint. Tailwind Traders is also a real Microsoft reference demo project (github.com/microsoft/TailwindTraders).
Verified by ci_pipeline_for_flask_api task setup scripts which make API calls to /TailwindTraders/_apis/git/repositories/TailwindTraders/. Repository path confirmed in export_result.ps1 scripts.

Specified in task.json description: 'Create a new CI pipeline named Tailwind-CI'. Cannot independently verify this value as no setup/export scripts exist for this task, but the claim is internally consistent with the task description.

Azure Pipelines CI badge markdown format verified via poanchen.github.io blog post and Microsoft Learn documentation. Full format is: [![Build Status](https://dev.azure.com/{org}/{project}/_apis/build/status/{repo}?branchName={branch})](URL). The expected_badge_text matches the opening of this standard markdown format. Official Azure Pipelines status badge documentation confirms this format at learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/pipelines/create-first-pipeline.

Verified as the standard Azure DevOps Server API base URL pattern. Matches the login URL format shown in ci_pipeline_for_flask_api task (http://localhost/DefaultCollection). Also consistent with Azure DevOps Server 2022 Express setup in env.json.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'establish_ci_badge_visibility' operates on the TailwindTraders project and repository within an Azure DevOps Server 2022 Express environment. The metadata specifies correct project/repo names that align with a real Microsoft reference demo project. The CI pipeline to be created is named 'Tailwind-CI', and its status badge should be added to the README.md file. The expected badge text format '[![Build Status]' matches the standard Azure Pipelines CI badge markdown format. The API base URL 'http://localhost/DefaultCollection' is the correct Azure DevOps Server local instance URL. The data operated on (Flask inventory API files) is synthetic but plausibly represents the repository contents. All metadata claims have been independently verified against authoritative sources including Microsoft documentation and other task setup scripts in the same environment.
